instructions.instruction: Use fetch-models.sh script to query available models instead of relying on OpenCode's built-in registry
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER rely on OpenCode's model registry alone...OpenCode's registry may be stale...use this skill's fetch-models.sh script to query GitHub Copilot API directly
instructions.relevant_when: user wants to see what models are available or verify model availability

instructions.instruction: Filter for policy.state === 'enabled' models only — never select models with disabled or preview state without checking
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER ignore policy.state when selecting models...filter for 'policy.state': 'enabled' models only: jq '.data[] | select(.policy.state == "enabled")'
instructions.relevant_when: listing or selecting a model for use

instructions.instruction: Always verify model availability via API before hardcoding a model ID in config or scripts
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER hardcode model IDs without verifying availability first...run './scripts/fetch-models.sh --json | jq .data[].id' first, verify model in output, then configure.
instructions.relevant_when: setting a default model in config or recommending a specific model ID

instructions.instruction: Check .capabilities.limits.max_context_window_tokens explicitly — never infer context window size from model name
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER assume context window size from model name...check .capabilities.limits.max_context_window_tokens explicitly in API response.
instructions.relevant_when: selecting a model for large-context tasks or comparing models by context window

instructions.instruction: Use --json flag with jq to filter and compare models programmatically
instructions.relevant_when: filtering models by capability or extracting model IDs for scripting

instructions.instruction: Authenticate via ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json — re-run opencode auth add github-copilot if 401 errors appear
instructions.original_snippets: AUTH_TOKEN=$(jq -r '.['github-copilot'].access' ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json)...If authentication fails: opencode auth add github-copilot
instructions.relevant_when: running fetch-models.sh or making manual API calls

instructions.instruction: Use per-command model override (--model flag) for testing new models before setting as project default
instructions.original_snippets: Per-command (recommended for testing): Use when trying new models...Set project default in .opencode/opencode.json only after confirming model stability
instructions.relevant_when: switching or trying a different model

instructions.instruction: Use category tags for selection guidance: 'powerful' for complex tasks, 'versatile' for general use, 'fast' for quick iterations
instructions.original_snippets: Category tags: Use powerful for complex tasks, versatile for general use, fast for quick iterations
instructions.relevant_when: helping a user choose between models

instructions.instruction: Use --picker-only flag to show only featured models available in the model picker
instructions.relevant_when: listing models that are prominently supported/recommended

instructions.instruction: Cross-check OpenCode models registry against the live API when troubleshooting 'models not showing' issues
instructions.original_snippets: Models not showing in OpenCode — OpenCode's registry may be stale; use the script to query the API directly.
instructions.relevant_when: troubleshooting model availability or discrepancy between what OpenCode shows and what's accessible
